actionable_feedback: Add an exported formula-view worksheet (or a separate CSV/TSV listing) that explicitly shows each computed cell formula and the cell references mapping back to the embedded packet values (R1..R4 and options O1..O3). This removes verifier friction when spreadsheet renderers are unavailable.

Embed an explicit budget-check cell in the workbook that references option cost cells and evaluates the boolean (total_cost <= remediation_budget_usd) so automated checks can validate feasibility without manual inspection.

Attach a worked validation example inside the workbook (and mirrored in the memo) showing the full formula-level calculation for one sensitivity perturbation (recommend R3 +5%) with cell references and resulting recomputed priority and classification.

Reconcile grader metadata: either remove/clear the semantic_score fields if semantic grading was intentionally omitted, or document the basis and evidence for the semantic_score in the grader metadata (so verifiers can trust the claim).

Ensure the SRE memo includes a short entry titled "How to verify workbook offline" listing the exact steps a human verifier should take (open formula view, recompute one perturbation, check budget cell).

feedback_summary: The baseline attempt produced the required deliverables and a clear SRE memo. Deterministic precheck and model grader passed and the verifier accepted the attempt with two warnings: (1) a rendering limitation (openpyxl and other spreadsheet renderers were unavailable) prevented visual inspection of the XLSX; verification relied on OOXML inspection reported in the agent trace, and (2) an internal consistency issue where deterministic_precheck metadata says semantic_correctness_not_evaluated while other outputs include semantic_score=1.0. Artifacts are traceable to the embedded packet, sensitivity testing is documented, and the recommended remediation (O1 + O2) and cost accounting match the actual_outputs.json.
